behavior_summary: This script implements the 'U8P_FIREWOOD::look' (examine) handler for the FIREWOOD item. When an actor inspects the firewood, the routine constructs the literal string "firewood " and asks the item/process system to announce that text to the inspecting process.

Before invoking the announcement, the routine pushes several call modifiers (including a numeric flag 0x208 and what disassembly shows as 'set info' and 'process exclude') which influence how the bark is delivered.
After calling Item::bark the routine consumes/frees the temporary string and performs a small implies/pop bookkeeping sequence, then suspends and returns without modifying any global game flags.
notable_control_flow: Triggered by an Event 0 'U8P_FIREWOOD::look' entry (area/inspect trigger).
Constructs and pushes the static string "firewood ", then sets call modifiers (0x208, set-info, process-exclude), pushes item/process and target pid, and calls Item::bark.

Handles the return from Item::bark by storing the return value in a local (retval), uses an 'implies' opcode with that retval, performs a pop into a temporary local, then suspends; no further branching or global state changes are present.
open_questions: The exact semantics of the numeric call flag 0x208 and the precise effects of the 'set info' / 'process exclude' modifiers in this context are not explicit in the disassembly and are inferred from pattern — appears to affect delivery scope and item processing (confidence: medium).
BP+06 is inferred to be the inspecting process/player pid (target for the bark) but the calling convention isn't directly documented here — this remains a likely inference (confidence: medium).

The 'implies' + pop sequence after Item::bark is a small control/bookkeeping idiom whose higher-level gameplay effect (if any) is unclear — it may be engine-level suspension/return handling (confidence: low).

main_function.name: U8P_FIREWOOD::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description/announcement for a firewood item when it is examined by an actor: produce the literal text "firewood " and route it to the inspecting process via Item::bark.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_FIREWOOD::look) — called when an actor/player looks at or examines the firewood item (area/inspect trigger).

function_map.name: U8P_FIREWOOD::look
function_map.role: Handles the Event 0 'U8P_FIREWOOD::look' for the FIREWOOD item; prepares description text and invokes the item bark to show it to the inspecting process.

external_calls.purpose: Cause the item to 'bark' (emit/display) the constructed string "firewood " to a target process/player. Called with the item/process reference, the string, the inspecting process pid, and call modifiers (0x208, set-info, process-exclude) that likely control delivery behavior.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the 'U8P_FIREWOOD::look' (examine) handler for the FIREWOOD item. When an actor inspects the firewood, the routine constructs the literal string "firewood " and asks the item/process system to announce that text to the inspecting process.
